narrativeFunction: Transform Sable from an isolated engineer running routine maintenance into a person harboring humanity's first contact — one she can't prove and won't report. By chapter end, something has spoken through her drone, and she has chosen silence.
dominantRegister: Technical precision eroding into uncertainty. Station-log formality giving way to fragments. The language should mirror Sable's composure — controlled, then cracking.
pacingTarget: Three-scene arc. Scene 1 is slow — routine, isolation, the comfortable weight of solitude. Scene 2 tightens — the signal has structure, her tools can't explain it. Scene 3 breaks — something speaks, and she can't explain it away.
endingPosture: Sable alone with a maintenance log she's falsified and an audio file she can't stop replaying. The last image is a choice: she did not file a maintenance ticket.

wrongAbout: That this is a standard day-in-the-life space story
That contact will be dramatic and unambiguous

knows: Sable is alone on Meridian station, day 340+
An anomalous signal appeared with internal mathematical structure
Something spoke through Drone Six's speaker using fragments of Sable's own language
Sable has not reported any of this
suspects: Echo is learning from Sable's logs
Echo has been listening longer than Sable has been analyzing
Sable won't report this because she doesn't want it to stop
Sable's isolation has compromised her objectivity
wrongAbout: That Echo is simply repeating — it's recombining
activeTensions: First contact is happening and only one person knows
The protocol violation is now potentially catastrophic
Echo is learning faster than Sable realizes
